name: ExtensionRequestOrchestration_Action_Transform
title: R5: Transform to apply the template (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`RequestOrchestration.action.transform` 0..1 `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/StructureMap)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` 0..1 `canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/StructureMap)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` has a context of RequestGroup.action based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `RequestGroup`.
Element `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Transform to apply the template (new)
element.comment:
Element `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` has a context of RequestGroup.action based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `RequestGroup`.
Element `RequestOrchestration.action.transform` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Note that when a referenced ActivityDefinition also defines a transform, the transform specified here generally takes precedence. In addition, if both a transform and dynamic values are specific, the dynamic values are applied to the result of the transform.

element.definition: A reference to a StructureMap resource that defines a transform that can be executed to produce the intent resource using the ActivityDefinition instance as the input.
element.comment: Note that when a referenced ActivityDefinition also defines a transform, the transform specified here generally takes precedence. In addition, if both a transform and dynamic values are specific, the dynamic values are applied to the result of the transform.
